channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Deals for QBs Darnold and Fields, pass rushers Williams and Sweat highlight start of NFL free agency
item.description: The NFL champion Philadelphia Eagles are losing two of their defensive disruptors who pressured Patrick Mahomes in the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Panthers' Aaron Ekblad suspended 20 games for violating NHL's performance-enhancing drug policy
item.description: Florida Panthers defenseman Aaron Ekblad was suspended 20 games without pay Monday for violating the NHL and NHL Players' Association's policy on performance-enhancing drugs, taking him off the ice for the defending Stanley Cup champions' stretch run and start of the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Yankees ace Gerrit Cole to have Tommy John surgery
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Yankees ace Gerrit Cole will have season-ending Tommy John surgery on his right elbow on Tuesday. The Yankees said the 34-year-old right-hander was examined Monday by Dr. Neal ElAttrache at the Cedars-Sinai Kerlan-Jobe Institute in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Eddie George relishes challenge in taking over at Bowling Green
item.description: BOWLING GREEN, Ohio (AP) — Eddie George was pondering the next steps in his burgeoning career as a college football coach while driving back from the NFL scouting combine. He got a sign in the most unlikely of places.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: A person with knowledge of the deal tells The Associated Press that the New York Jets and former Pittsburgh Steelers quarterback Justin Fields have agreed to a two-year contract worth $40 million.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Waszak Jr., Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UCLA and South Carolina are 1-2 in women's AP Top 25; No. 6 TCU has highest ranking in its history
item.description: UCLA vaulted back up to No. 1 in The Associated Press women's basketball Top 25 on Monday after beating USC for the Big Ten title over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seahawks agree to sign quarterback Sam Darnold to 3-year deal: reports
item.description: The Seahawks reportedly agreed to sign Sam Darnold to a three-year deal, replacing Geno Smith after Darnold led the Vikings to the playoffs last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: QB Sam Darnold agrees on a 3-year, $100.5 million deal with Seahawks, AP source says
item.description: Quarterback Sam Darnold has agreed on a three-year, $100.5 million contract with the Seattle Seahawks, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi And Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Khalil Mack staying with Chargers. Deal is for 1 year, $18 million, AP sources say
item.description: Khalil Mack will remain with the Los Angeles Chargers after agreeing to a one-year deal on Monday. Mack's $18 million salary for the 2025 season will be fully guaranteed according to two people familiar with the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bills reward Allen with new contract after NFL MVP season. Deal is worth $330M, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — NFL MVP Josh Allen was rewarded Sunday with a contract extension worth $330 million, including an NFL record $250 million of it guaranteed, which makes him among the league’s highest-paid players.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vikings bring back Aaron Jones after successful first season in Minnesota
item.description: Aaron Jones is staying with the Minnesota Vikings as the team announced agreeing to terms on a deal reportedly worth $20 million over the next two seasons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boys Rugby: Cathedral Catholic 28, La Costa Canyon 17
item.description:
The High School Boys Rugby Championship was held in Del Mar as the Cathedral Catholic Dons hosted the La Costa Canyon Mavericks.
It was the Dons who came out victorious, defeated La Costa Canyon by a score of 28-17, to win their 5th straight Rugby Championship.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Allende scores to lead Miami past Charlotte 1-0 with Messi again watching from the bench
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Tadeo Allende scored 17 seconds into the second half and Inter Miami won its third straight match without Lionel Messi, beating Charlotte FC 1-0 on Sunday. Messi has not played since a win over Sporting Kansas City on Feb.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees GM Brian Cashman 'prepared for the worst' with Gerrit Cole's elbow issue
item.description: Gerrit Cole's elbow injury has New York Yankees GM Brian Cashman 'prepared for the worst,' just like the fan base, as they await results from imaging and second opinions
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL legend Eddie George agrees to become Bowling Green's next head coach job: report
item.description: Eddie George, a Heisman Trophy winner, spent the past four years as the Tennessee State coach. But he is leaving to take the head coaching job at Bowling Green, per reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bills release Von Miller in salary cap-saving move: Report
item.description: The move frees up at least $8.4 million in salary cap space, which comes close to matching how much the team was projected to be over the limit.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Daytona 500 winner William Byron claims NASCAR Cup pole
item.description: On Saturday, Daytona 500 winner William Byron claimed the Busch Light pole during the 312-mile race at Phoenix Raceway. According to NASCAR, it's the 14th pole of the 27 year-old's career.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs WR Xavier Worthy released after prosecutor declines charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago visits Miami after White's 44-point game
item.description: Chicago Bulls (25-38, 10th in the Eastern Conference) vs. Miami Heat (29-33, seventh in the Eastern Conference) Miami; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah's comeback falls short in 4-3 overtime loss
item.description: CHICAGO (ABC4 Sports) - Connor Bedard scored at 3:10 of overtime to give the Chicago Blackhawks a 4-3 victory against the Utah Hockey Club at United Center on Friday. Bedard circled the net and scored from the slot after going eight games without a goal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Girls Basketball: Mater Dei Catholic 59, Carlsbad 52
item.description: Mater Dei Catholic winning and advancing to the Division III State Regional semifinals, 59-52 over Carlsbad,
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Yanni Gourde back where he belongs, in Tampa Bay
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning organization believes that adding two elite forwards Yanni Gourde and Oliver Byorkstrand will be the push they need to make a deep playoff run.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC
